title: World Devastator
content:

|lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems=[[Molecular furnace]] |availability= |role=*Superweapon *Automated factory |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation= *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Dark Empire]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] {{C|captured}}''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' }} {{Quote|This is the greatest scourge the galaxy has ever seen… Far more lethal than the Death Star!|Admiral Gial Ackbar|[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]}} '''World Devastators''', also known as '''World Smashers''', '''World Sweepers''', '''Planet Smashers''', or '''City Eaters''', were gigantic [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] with strong [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] attached to them that could wreak havoc on [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. They were designed by [[Umak Leth]] and employed by the [[Dark Empire]] led by the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Great fires burn in its belly…and everything it eats is transformed into new instruments of doom.|Umak Leth|[[Dark Empire endnotes|''Dark Empire'' endnotes]]}} [[File:World_Dev.jpg|thumb|left|150px|World Devastators over Mon Calamari]] Although it is debatable if the World Devastators were deadlier than the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], they were undoubtedly more efficient. The Death Star could merely destroy worlds, while the World Devastators could use a targeted world's material resources for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] benefit. A Devastator's primary tactic was to land on the surface of a planet. There, its mighty tractor beam projectors would literally tear the planet beneath it apart, thus making them "planet killers." The World Devastators functioned similar to the [[Star Forge]], which also harvested materials from a nearby astronomical object.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' The deconstruction of the planet and its resources would then be used for both fuel for the Devastator and for its massive foundries which churned out [[Starship/Legends|starships]], [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and [[Droid/Legends|droids]] which would be sent into battle straight from the assembly line. The internal factories could create anything from a starfighter or speeder to a medium-sized [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], given enough resources. In this way the World Devastators functioned similar to the [[Star Forge]], a massive automated shipyard created by the [[Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] which also harvested materials from a nearby astronomical object for the purpose of constructing military equipment. Each World Devastator was controlled by a central droid brain—the sentient crewmembers were aboard to oversee production and handle navigation or weapons systems. The droid brain was responsible for creating the new weapons of war, and was programmed with a massive store of ship types and parameters, able to create a wide variety of vessels for any combat situation. In addition, the factories could also focus on upgrading the World Devastator itself; a portion of the raw materials created in the [[molecular furnace]] was set aside for advancements to the main body. With these unique customizations, no two "mature" World Devastators were truly alike. Given sufficient time and resources, the World Devastators could even manufacture more World Devastators. These machines' [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] were so strong that even concentrated [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire could not penetrate them, and most times any contact with another enemy ship would result in the consumption of that ship by the World Devastator. Three smaller Devastators carried their shield generators on their outer hull, which were vulnerable to starfighter attack. If two of their four "legs" were destroyed, the Devastator would fall to the surface without their power.''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' ==History==
[[File:World_Devastators.jpg|thumb|200px|World Devastators ravage the surface of Mon Calamari.]]
The Devastator design went through development in [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] [[Maw Installation|Installation]] research center prior to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first]] [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] completion, along with other [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]], like the [[Sun Crusher]]. However, they were developed in secret Imperial [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] with a vague outline of the project discussed in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', with [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] believing circa [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] that they never made it out of the planning stages.''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'' The earliest operational World Devastators were deployed in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], where rumors escaped to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] of terrible "city-smashers" destroying worlds. Shortly thereafter, fleets of World Devastators appeared around dozens of Republic-affiliated worlds. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Comeg]]'s [[Comeg's fleet|fleet]] located in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], held a prototype World Devastator, ''[[Silencer-7]]''.[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]] They saw heavy opposition in the [[Battle of Calamari|Battle of Mon Calamari]], where they were used against the ocean world of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], the temporary capital of the New Republic after the [[Imperial Coalition|remnants of the Empire]] [[Second Battle of Coruscant|recaptured]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The ocean world of Mon Calamari had once been slated for destruction by the first Death Star. With the destruction of that battle station—and its successor—it seemed as if Mon Calamari was free of the Empire's grip. But when the reborn Palpatine launched his attack, Mon Calamari became the first target of his World Devastators. Led by the massive ''Silencer-7'', the Devastator squad descended on Mon Calamari, smashing the floating cities and shipyards, feeding their factories in anticipation of a New Republic counterstrike. These foundries would produce various assault vehicles including [[TIE/D automated starfighter|TIE/D fighters]] and [[AQ-5 Waveskimmer|wavewalkers]]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] sent a New Republic fleet to deal with the devastating attack on his homeworld, including the captured [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' and a squadron of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]. Yet nothing the New Republic threw at the Devastators could stop their inexorable march. [[File:WorldDevastator-HBDE.jpg|thumb|A World Devastator tearing apart a Mon Calamari city.]] The World Devastators launched automated droid TIE fighters against the Republic starfighters, and one Devastator headed into orbit to face ''Emancipator'' head-on. The Devastator devoured the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] in its boiling furnace, forcing the crew to evacuate or become the metal monster's next meal. Luckily, the Rebels had another force in reserve, and sent [[V-wing airspeeder|V-wing]] and [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing]] squadrons to the surface to engage the Devastators. [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] attacked and disabled three smaller Devastators. The squadron's actions saved nearby Mon Calamari cities.[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] [[File:Worlddevs.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Schematics of a World Devastator]] During the battle, the assault weapons stopped dead in the water. While the Alliance believed the Imperial commander was too inept to even claim victory with the Devastators, this sudden turn of events was entirely to plan. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] had infiltrated the upper echelons of Imperial command by supposedly allying himself with Palpatine. From his position as [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Imperial Forces]], Skywalker was able to shut down the Devastators. Unfortunately, their internal factories continued churning out wavewalkers and TIE/D fighters, forcing the New Republic to implement a new plan while they fought the Imperial vehicles. Luke Skywalker smuggled a wealth of Imperial data into Rebel hands, via the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. The little droid was able to decipher the data, and create a new code that would interfere with the Devastators' droid brains. The virus worked wonders; the Devastators turned on each other, leaving nothing but dead hulks in the water world's oceans. The New Republic captured at least [[World Devastator (New Republic)|one World Devastator]], as one was among the fleet surrounding [[Da Soocha V]], eating up derelict spacecraft.''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' Any craft the Republic had was later scrapped after a public outcry.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' The destruction of the World Devastator fleet seemed to bother Emperor Palpatine very little, and unsurprisingly, for he soon unleashed a more terrifying weapon: the [[Galaxy Gun]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Mommawd.jpg|thumb|right|"Momma" Devastator concept art]] Plans were originally made to include super-sized "Momma Devastators" in ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', but the idea never went beyond concept art. The World Devastators seen in the video game [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] could be destroyed by precision attacks against them made by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron's]] V-wing airspeeders. As the ''Dark Empire'' incarnation of the Devastators were repeatedly said to be virtually indestructible, this throws the notion of destructible Devastators (notably smaller in ''Rogue Squadron'' than in the comics) into a realm of uncertain canonicity. It is unknown whether the element of V-wings being able to destroy World Devastators, central to that level of the game, falls under the category of non-canon game mechanics or actually indicates that some of the smaller Devastators deployed at Dac were vulnerable to starfighter or airspeeder attack. The strategy guide for the game implied that the latter idea was the correct one. In the article ''[[Dark Sides]]'' for the RPG magazine ''[[Casus Belli (magazine)|Casus Belli]]'', authors [[Patrick Bousquet]] and [[Christophe Debien]] envisioned the [[Imperial Leviathan]] as a predecessor to the Word Devastators. Yet, that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, and thus is considered non-canon by [[Lucasfilm]].{{CasusBelliCite|90|Dark Sides}} In the online game ''[[Death Star Designer]]'', one can add a World Devastator to their Death Star design. In [[Canon]], the role of a planet destroying weapons used by the resurrected [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]] were given to the [[Xyston-class Star Destroyer]]. In very early development on [[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker]], a "Planet Eater", which would have terraformed the exterior of planets into an industrial surface, was in consideration for the "Big Bad".[https://www.artstation.com/artwork/mqrOBy Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker - "Planet Eater" sketches (2017)] ==Appearances== *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] {{Mo}} *[[Star
